home.title: $t(global.gameName) - Free Idle MMO Game - Gather, Craft, Battle, Trade, and Cows!
home.subtitle: Multiplayer Idle RPG
home.bannerText: Embark on a journey through the $t(global.gameName) universe, a unique multiplayer idle game. Whether you enjoy resource gathering, item crafting, or engaging in epic battles against alien monsters, we have something to offer for everyone. Immerse yourself in our thriving community, where you can trade in the player-driven marketplace, form a guild with friends, chat with fellow players, or climb to the top of the leaderboards!

labyrinthPanel.levelLocked: The Labyrinth unlocks at total level {{requiredLevel}}.
labyrinthPanel.instructions:
The Labyrinth consists of floors of skilling and combat challenges. Each run costs 1 entry. 1 entry regenerates every 2-3 days depending on cooldown upgrades.
  • Recommended: Levels in many different skills and combat styles to attempt at least half of the room types to effectively run the Labyrinth.
  • Floors & Rooms: Each floor is a grid of rooms. Clear rooms to reveal adjacent ones and find the floor exit to advance. Difficulty increases as you advance through more floors.
  • Pathing: Click rooms to queue a path. Rooms are automatically challenged in order. Drag to select multiple rooms at once.
  • Room Types: Skilling rooms challenge one of 10 non-combat skills. Combat rooms pit you against one of 10 labyrinth monsters. Treasure rooms grant item rewards. The floor exit grants larger rewards and unlocks the next floor.
  • Torches: One torch is consumed per room entered. Produced with Crafting.
  • Shrouds: Instantly clear a visible challenge room without receiving XP or rewards from it. Produced with Tailoring.
  • Beacons: Reveal hidden rooms in an area. Produced with Cheesesmithing.
  • Crates: Grant buffs that last the entire run. One crate of each type is consumed on entry. Tea and Coffee Crates are produced with Brewing, Food Crates with Cooking.
  • Rewards: Earn Labyrinth Tokens, Purdora's Boxes, Labyrinth Refinement Chests (Floor 6+), and XP in the relevant skill. Rewards increase with floor level. Spend tokens in the Labyrinth Shop on upgrades and items.
  • Automation: Set loadouts that will be automatically equipped for different room types and auto-skip thresholds. Upgrade Full Automation from the shop to auto-path through floors.

tutorialQuest.welcomeStartText:
Welcome to $t(global.gameName) - a world with magical cows!

I'm Purple, the Chief Training Officer (CTO), and also your tour guide today!

I'll cast an orange glow to guide you through the training.
tutorialQuest.milkCowStartText:
Let me first show you what we magical cows are best known for: producing magical milk! By the way, my cousin Burble also works here. Hi Burble!

First, try and gather some milk.
tutorialQuest.milkCowCompleteText: Good job! Here's some extra milk and a brush. Magical cows love to be brushed, and happy cows produce milk faster!
tutorialQuest.smithCheeseStartText: Let's make some cheese with the milk! These special cheeses are very durable and can be turned into many useful things through cheesesmithing!
tutorialQuest.smithCheeseCompleteText: Great! Take some extra cheese with you for the next task.
tutorialQuest.smithSwordStartText: Cheeses are essential resources for making tools, weapons, and armor. Let me show you how to make a cheese sword. I know it might sound crazy and maybe a little bit smelly too, but trust me.
tutorialQuest.smithSwordCompleteText: Awesome! As you level up, equipment can be upgraded to higher tiers! Tools can also be made to improve each of your skills.
tutorialQuest.forageFarmlandStartText: Now let's go forage for some more resources. Head to Farmland and see what items you can gather!
tutorialQuest.forageFarmlandCompleteText: That was fast! Foraging gives you resources used in many skills, including cooking, brewing, and tailoring!
tutorialQuest.cookDonutStartText: It's time to make use of your cooking skill and whip up a delicious donut using some eggs, wheat, and sugar. What? You can't cook? Of course you can! There's a rat from Earth that can cook, and if he can do it, so can you! Give it a try!
tutorialQuest.cookDonutCompleteText: Fantastic! Food can heal you while in combat. Here's a dozen more donuts for free!
tutorialQuest.fightFlyStartText: Now I want to take you on an expedition to one of our neighboring planets - the Smelly Planet! I hear there are lots of flies, and they bite! You'll want to bring your sword and some donuts. Let's go!
tutorialQuest.fightFlyCompleteText:
Battling monsters earns you coins, resources, ability books, and even rare items.

If you are knocked out during combat, you will recover in 150 seconds and continue fighting.
tutorialQuest.messageTipsStartText:
Looks like the tour is almost over. There's still much more to explore, but don't worry, you won't be alone! Once you level up a little more, you can chat with or get help from other players!

You can also buy or sell items in our player-driven marketplace, unless you are playing Ironcow mode.
tutorialQuest.messageTips2StartText:
Before I go, here's a few more tips:
- A Game Guide can be found at the bottom of the navigation menu on the left.
- If you go offline, you'll continue to make progress for 10 hours (upgradable).
- Items, abilities, skills, and enemies can be hovered over (long press on mobile) to see more detailed tooltips.

I have to go now. It's time for my second lunch, and I have four stomachs to fill. Now go explore the Milky Way!

chat.reportInstructions: Submit a report for serious chat disruptions or rule violations. For minor personal disagreements, please use the block feature instead.

reportTypeDescription.harassmentMe: Personal attacks or harassment directed at me.
reportTypeDescription.harassmentOthers: Personal attacks or harassment directed at someone else.
reportTypeDescription.offensiveLanguage: Use of offensive or inappropriate language.
reportTypeDescription.illegalViolentSexual: Message, discussion, or URL link that is illegal, excessively violent, or sexual in nature.
reportTypeDescription.controversialTopics: Divisive topics such as politics, religion, international conflicts, gender discussions, sexuality, mute/ban complaints, or other topics that frequently become disruptive.
reportTypeDescription.excessiveDrama: Excessive drama or heated arguments that disrupt the chat.
reportTypeDescription.spam: Single player spamming chat with meaningless messages, excessive duplicate messages, or begging others for free items. (Multiple players posting similar messages is not considered spam)
reportTypeDescription.encouragingRuleBreaking: Encouraging or misleading other players into breaking game rules.

guideTooltip.milkingContent:
The milks from these magical cows have a wide variety of functions. They can be used to produce consumables or craft into special cheese to make equipment.
Cows love to be brushed. Equipping a brush will boost your milking skill.
guideTooltip.foragingContent:
You can find many different resources while foraging in the various areas. These resources can be used for cooking and brewing consumables.
Equipping shears will boost your foraging skill.
guideTooltip.woodcuttingContent:
You can gather logs from different types of trees. Logs are used for crafting various equipments.
Equipping a hatchet will boost your woodcutting skill.
guideTooltip.cheesesmithingContent:
The hardened cheeses made with milks from the magical cows are as tough as metal. You can smith them into equipment that gives you bonuses in combat or skilling.
Equipment is upgradable from one tier to the next, often requiring increasing amount of cheese. There is also special equipment that can be crafted with items found from monsters in combat.
Equipping a hammer will boost your cheesesmithing skill.
guideTooltip.craftingContent:
You can craft weapons, offhands, and jewelry.
Equipping a chisel will boost your crafting skill.
guideTooltip.tailoringContent:
You can tailor ranged and magic clothing using raw materials gathered from combat and foraging.
Equipping a needle will boost your tailoring skill.
guideTooltip.cookingContent:
Food can be used to recover your HP or MP. They can be brought with you to combat.
Equipping a spatula will boost your cooking skill.
guideTooltip.brewingContent:
Drinks can provide you with temporary buffs. Coffee can be brought with you to combat and tea can be used while skilling.
Equipping a pot will boost your brewing skill.
guideTooltip.alchemyContent:
Alchemy allows you to transform items into other items. Each alchemy action has a different success rate, and the input items will always be consumed regardless of success or failure.
Coinify: Converts item into coins. Decompose: Converts item into component materials. Transmute: Converts item into a random related item, and in some cases unique items that cannot be acquired elsewhere. Unrefine: Converts refined item back to its base version, returning some refinement shards.
Each transformation has a base success rate. The success rate is lower if your alchemy level is lower than the item level. Catalyst and tea can be used to increase the success rate.
Equipping an Alembic will boost your alchemy skill.
guideTooltip.enhancingContent:
Enhancing allows you to permanently improve your equipment, giving them increasing bonuses as their enhancement level go up.
Enhancing costs a small amount of materials for each attempt. The success rate depends on your enhancing skill level, the tier of the equipment, and the equipment's current enhancement level. You gain 0.05% enhancing success bonus for each level you have above the item's recommended level. A successful enhancement will increase the level by 1 and failure will reset the level back to 0.
You can optionally use copies of the base equipment for protection. Failure with protection will only reduce the enhancement level by 1 but consume 1 protection item.
Equipping an enhancer will boost your enhancing success.
guideTooltip.combatContent:
Fighting monsters will earn you experience and item drops. Your combat stats are based on a combination of your combat skill levels and your equipment bonuses.
You can bring food to recover HP or MP, drinks to give you buffs, and abilities that can be cast. You can change the automation configuration from the settings icon below them.
If you are defeated in combat, your character will wait through a respawn timer before automatically continuing combat.
guideTooltip.marketplaceContent:
The marketplace allows players to make buy or sell listings for any tradable item. You can click on any item listed to view existing listings or to create your own.
New listings will always be fulfilled by the best matching prices on market when possible. If no immediate fulfillment is possible, the listing will appear on the marketplace.
When a trade is successful, a tax of 2% coins is taken and the received items can be collected from "My Listings" tab.
Asks: existing sell listings.
Bids: existing buy listings.
guideTooltip.combatStatsContent:
Attack Interval: How fast you can auto-attack.
Ability Haste: Reduces ability cooldown.
Accuracy: Increases chance to successfully attack.
Damage: Auto-attack damage is random between 1 and the maximum damage.
Critical Hit: Always rolls maximum damage. Ranged style has passive critical chance.
Task Damage: Increases damage to monsters assigned as tasks.
Amplify: Increases damage of that type.
Evasion: Increases chance to dodge an attack.
Armor: Mitigates % of physical damage.
Resistance: Mitigates % of elemental damage.
Penetration: Ignores % of armor/resistance.
Life Steal: Heal for % of auto-attack.
Mana Leech: Leeches for % of auto-attack.
Thorn: When attacked, deals a percentage of your defensive damage back to the attacker. Damage is increased by 1% per armor or resistance.
Retaliation: When attacked, reflects a percentage of (defensive damage + incoming damage) as a smash attack back to the attacker.
Tenacity: Reduces chance of being blinded, silenced, or stunned.
Threat: Increases chance of being targeted by monsters.
Regen: Recover % of Max HP/MP per 10s.
Food Haste: Reduces food cooldown.
Drink Concentration: Increases drink effect. Reduces duration and cooldown.
guideTooltip.noncombatStatsContent:
Speed: Increases action speed
Task Speed: Increases speed on actions assigned as tasks.
Gathering Quantity: Increases gathering quantity
Efficiency: Chance of repeating the action instantly
Skilling Essence Find: Increases chance of finding essences
Skilling Rare Find: Increase rare item drop rate.
guideTooltip.abilitiesContent:
Abilities can be learned from ability books. You can acquire ability books as drops from monsters or purchase them from other players in the marketplace.
Abilities can be placed into slots to be used in combat. You unlock more slots as your intelligence skill level increases.
Abilities can level up as you gain experience. You get 0.1 experience for every use in combat and a much larger amount from consuming duplicate ability books.
guideTooltip.houseContent:
Rooms in your house can be built to give you permanent bonuses.
Each room can be leveled up to a maximum of level 8 with increasing costs.
guideTooltip.loadoutsContent:
Loadouts allow you to save your current equipment, consumables, and abilities to be automatically loaded later with actions. Loadouts can be tied to a single skill or "All Skills." Selecting "All Skills" will only save equipment.
Setting a loadout as default will auto-select the loadout when choosing any action in the skill(s) the loadout is associated with.
guideTooltip.enhancingProtectionContent: One protection item is consumed on failure to ensure that only 1 enhancement level is lost instead of being reset to 0. Philosopher's Mirror is always consumed and guarantees success.
guideTooltip.alchemyCatalystContent: Catalyst increases success rate. One catalyst is consumed per success.
guideTooltip.achievementsContent:
Achievements: Goals spanning many areas of the game. Completing all in a tier grants buffs for the character.
Collection: Tracks all items obtained outside of Marketplace and General Shop.
Bestiary: Tracks all monsters defeated. Higher tier monsters grant 1 extra credit per tier. Party combat gives fractional credit based on party size.

Each item/monster earns points based on how many you've collected/defeated (1→+1pt, 10→+2pt, 100→+3pt, etc).
guideTooltip.labyrinthContent:
The Labyrinth consists of floors of skilling and combat challenges. Bring torches, shrouds, beacons, and crates as supplies.
Earn Labyrinth Tokens to buy upgrades and rewards from the Labyrinth Shop.
guideTooltip.guildContent:
Guilds are groups of players who play, chat, and progress together.
Take part in weekly guild trials to earn Guild Points for guild buildings and Guild Tokens for personal shrine buffs.
Exchange items for guild credits to buy shrine buffs and unlock guild icons.

1783897200000.heading: Guild Expansion, Additional Localizations, and QOL Improvements
1783897200000.content:
The guild expansion has arrived! Guilds now run weekly Guild Trials: cooperative skilling and combat challenges where the whole guild climbs tiers of increasing difficulty together. Trials use a snapshot of your chosen loadout and run alongside your regular actions, so participating never interrupts your progress or uses up your supplies. Clearing tiers earns Guild Points for the guild and Guild Tokens for yourself, and when all trials finish for the week, every member receives bonus tokens based on the guild's overall progress.

Guild Points can be spent on 23 guild buildings and 5 shrines. Buildings expand member capacity, boost trial rewards, and strengthen members during trials, while shrines unlock Shrine Buffs, which are permanent personal buffs purchased with Guild Tokens and Guild Credits. Guild Credits are earned by exchanging items with your guild, and those exchanges also unlock guild icons as your guild's lifetime totals grow. The update also adds guild types (Casual, Standard, and Ironcow), public guild profiles with applications, a member contributions view, new guild leaderboard categories, and leadership claiming for guilds with inactive leaders. Check out the new Guild tab in the Game Guide for all the details.

In other news, the game is now officially available in 7 additional languages: Español, Français, Português, 繁體中文, 日本語, 한국어, and Русский. Every language went through extensive review with feedback from native speakers and players, and we want to thank everyone who contributed. Chat has better language support too: the new Chat Translation feature can automatically translate chat messages into your language, General chat has been renamed to Global alongside a new English language channel, and you can now join a second language channel.

This update also includes a number of QOL improvements. You can now reorder queued actions and loadouts with drag-and-drop, customize the order of the navigation menu, and track per-skill experience in the Loot & XP Log (previously the Loot Tracker). The Cowbell Store has some new additions as well, including two new cosmetic types (Avatar Backgrounds and Avatar Borders), equipment-themed avatar outfits, and the option to exchange Supporter Points for Cowbells.

Visit the Patch Notes for the full list of changes.

1777165200000.heading: Labyrinth Improvements, Steam Client Update, and More!
1777165200000.content:
We've made a number of improvements to the Labyrinth based on player feedback. The biggest change is that scrolls will no longer provide buffs in the Labyrinth, and instead, 9 new permanent buff upgrades are available in the Labyrinth Shop. Scrolls were originally designed to be used outside the Labyrinth, but as players started bringing them along, they became all but required for any serious run, adding significant cost to actively manage them. Permanent upgrades are healthier long-term: progress carries forward, there's no need to actively manage scroll usage, and your strength scales with time invested. Players will be slightly weaker until they accumulate enough new upgrades, but this is a one-time transition. To help bridge the gap, existing scrolls can now be decomposed into 5 Labyrinth Tokens each via Alchemy.

We've also added a 1000+ total level requirement to enter the Labyrinth for the first time. Looking at our data, only a handful of players below this level have engaged with the Labyrinth with reasonable success, and many newer players were getting confused or frustrated trying it too early. Gating the entry encourages newer players to focus on their core progression first and approach the Labyrinth when they're more prepared for it. Existing characters who have already entered the Labyrinth are unaffected.

For high-level players who can reach floor 12 or higher, we've heard feedback that Labyrinth runs take too much total time. The permanent buff upgrades will reduce overall run time by a moderate amount, and we'll continue to monitor this issue to determine if any additional adjustments are needed.

In other news, the Steam client's built-in extension manager has been completely redesigned. The new manager behaves much like a browser extension manager: paste a userscript URL to install or update an extension, and most browser-compatible scripts should run unchanged. This addresses the compatibility issues and manual setup overhead of the previous version.

We've also looked at some of the underperforming combat builds and decided to give Bulwark and Crossbow setups some buffs in this update.

Visit the Patch Notes for the full list of changes.

1771718400000.heading: Labyrinth and QOL Improvements
1771718400000.content:
The Labyrinth is here! Take on floors of skilling and combat challenges with increasing difficulty. Navigate through grids of rooms, bring supplies, earn Labyrinth Tokens, Purdora's Boxes, and Labyrinth Refinement Chests, and spend your tokens in the Labyrinth Shop on upgrades and unique new items including Skilling Capes, Combat Boots, and Scrolls. Set up per-room loadouts and full automation to optimize your runs, and compete on the Labyrinth Points leaderboard. Check out the Game Guide in-game for all the details.

This update also brings several highly requested QOL improvements. "Start Now" can now optionally insert actions to the front of your action queue instead of replacing it. Queue Party Ready can be queued after solo actions, allowing you to automatically return to party combat without needing to be present. Browser notifications are now available for those who play from Steam or browser, allowing you to be notified of in-game events (not available on mobile).

Lastly, we are introducing a creator code program for revenue sharing with partnered content creators. If you are a content creator and are interested in promoting the game, email contact@milkywayidle.com or contact us on Discord.

Visit the Patch Notes for more details.

1764979200000.heading: Achievements, Collection, Bestiary, And More!
1764979200000.content:
We are excited to introduce our latest feature allowing you to earn achievements for reaching various goals across many areas of the game. Completing full tiers of achievements grants permanent buffs, giving you more reasons to push for those goals. Track your progress in the new Achievements tab and show off your accomplishments on your profile.

Alongside achievements, we are launching the Collection and Bestiary features. Collection lets you track every item you have obtained, earn collect points, and compete on a new leaderboard. Bestiary tracks the monsters you have defeated.

We are also introducing the Philosopher's Mirror, a new enhancing item that allows you to more consistently upgrade your equipment at the highest enhancement levels. For example, a Philosopher's Mirror combined with a +14 item and a +13 item will guarantee a +15 item.

Lastly, we wanted to let everyone know that we will be ending custom cosmetic requests at the end of the year. While we have enjoyed creating personalized cosmetics for supporters, it has become a significant time investment that takes away from other development work. We will continue to accept requests until the end of the year! Supporter points can now be used for community buffs, and more use cases for supporter points are planned in the near future.

Visit the Patch Notes for more details.

1755558000000.heading: Combat Rework, Charms, and Refined Equipment
1755558000000.content:
This update introduces a major overhaul to the combat system, alongside new features such as charms, refined dungeon equipment, and expanded combat difficulty tiers.

Over time, we identified long-term balance issues between the three combat styles (Melee, Ranged, and Magic). These issues made it very difficult to balance experience and income for different combat styles and across different states of progression. Some of the biggest issues included:
  1. Uneven skill requirements: Melee players relies on an extra Attack skill, leading to imbalance in experience distribution and coffee boosts.
  2. Magic ability scaling: Because magic does not auto attack, magic damaging abilities were granting roughly twice as much DPS increase per level compared to other styles, making it hard to balance damage outputs fairly across all levels of progression.
  3. Experience imbalances: XP formulas unintentionally favored certain strategies, even when DPS was the same.

The rework addresses these issues and sets up combat for long-term balance. The key changes include:
  1. All combat styles now require Attack skill for accuracy. Players will immediately receive Attack XP based on 15% of their Ranged and 12% of Magic experience as it's needed to be viable. The attack experience ratio is based on leaderboard data to allow total experience to catch up with Melee.
  2. Magic ability damage scaling per level decreased to 0.5% per level.
  3. Additional bonuses are added to ranged and magic equipment to maintain similar damage output as before.
  4. Bulwark reworked and significantly increased damage output to be more comparable to other combat styles.
  5. Aura system rework with new scaling mechanics based on skill levels, and a one-time XP refund via books.
  6. New combat XP system tied to monsters defeated, with Charms allowing you to distribute training XP toward specific skills.
  7. Party system update: XP divided equally, with decreased experience and drops for large level differences.

Along with the combat rework, we are introducing additional combat difficulty tiers. Combat zones now scale from T0 up to T5, and the new charm equipment can be found in higher difficulty zones. T1 and T2 dungeons are also added, where refinement shards can be obtained to refine your T95 and back slot items.

Visit the Patch Notes for more details.

1747609200000.heading: MooPass and Other News
1747609200000.content:
The MooPass is here, an optional membership that provides a variety of helpful but non-essential perks! All characters can claim 14 days of MooPass for free, and an additional 14 days have been granted to all existing characters. For more details, check out the Patch Notes or directly in Cowbell Store.

As with any new form of monetization, we understand there are concerns about the game's future direction and the potential for pay-to-win. We want to reassure you that we have no plans to introduce any monetization that could be considered predatory. We've carefully considered community feedback and designed the MooPass to be affordable and fair for both paying and free-to-play players. It introduces no content paywalls and has no impact on core gameplay, ensuring a level playing field. By offering recurring value, MooPass also helps maintain demand for Cowbells beyond one-time convenience upgrades and cosmetics, which supports the long-term sustainability of Milky Way Idle.

In other news, the continued growth of our playerbase has posed new challenges for our moderation team. Our current system depends on volunteer moderators actively monitoring the in-game chat. We've seen feedback both regarding moderation strictness and inappropriate messages being missed during low-coverage times. To address this, we're developing a new system that will rely more heavily on player reports, giving players more control over their chat experience. For example, when moderators are unavailable, messages that receive enough reports will be subject to automated moderation. This also allows our moderators to take a less proactive role and instead focus on messages that have been flagged by the community. We expect to release this system within the next week or two.

Lastly, a reminder to follow our rules regarding the single-account policy and trade boosting. With many new players joining, some may not be familiar with our stance on funenlng wealth from alt accounts or coin purchases from gold sellers. We regularly conduct ban waves. For example, over the past couple of weeks, we've issued temporary or permanent bans to 500+ main accounts and thousands of alt accounts. Players receiving temp bans also had 2–3x the boosted wealth removed, totaling over 100 billion coins. Cheating is not worth the risk, and we will continue to improve our detection systems.

1744930800000.heading: Pirate Cove Dungeon, 4th Character Slot, and Custom Cosmetic Policy Update
1744930800000.content:
The Pirate Cove Dungeon is now open for exploration! This new dungeon features a variety of new T95 weapons and armors, as well as new abilities. We've also made some adjustments to existing items and abilities to improve balance and gameplay. Check out the full patch notes for all the details!

In other news, we've added a new 4th character slot for players, allowing everyone the same opportunity of having up to 1 Standard and 3 Ironcow characters.

We are also updating our custom cosmetic policy and pricing. While the feature started as nice gift to show our appreciation to our supporters, it's now too overwhelming with the huge increase in playerbase. We've spent 150-200 hours to work on about 100 requests in the past month, and we need to be able to allocate more time for other development tasks. Starting May 1st, custom cosmetics will require spending supporter points and cowbells. Any requests initiated before the end of April will be granted based on the previous supporter point requirements.

1734825600000.heading: Skilling Expansion Part 2 - Celestial Tools and Skilling Outfits
1734825600000.content: We're excited to release the second part of the skilling expansion, introducing Celestial Tools and Skilling Outfits! After using Holy tools for such a long time, skilling specialists can finally get their hands on some upgrades. These items are not easy to acquire, but those who are dedicated (or wealthy) enough to obtain them will be rewarded with a significant boost. Dive in and take your skilling to the next level!

1783897200000.heading: Major Patch - Guild Expansion, Additional Localizations, and QOL Improvements
1783897200000.content:
Guilds:
  1. Guild Trials: weekly cooperative challenges for the whole guild.
    1. Each week your guild receives 4 random skilling trials and 2 random combat trials. The guild week resets every Friday at 00:00 UTC.
    2. Each member can sign up for one skilling and one combat trial. Trials start automatically at the scheduled time (skilling first, then combat), each lasting up to an hour.
    3. Trials run alongside your regular actions without interrupting them, using a snapshot of your selected loadout. No consumables are used.
    4. Trials climb tiers starting at level 100, with each tier 10 levels higher and progressively harder, up to level 300.
    5. Combat trials feature 5 encounter types: Badger, Chameleon, Jellyfish, Hedgehog, and Swarm. Difficulty scales with the number of participants.
    6. Clearing tiers earns Guild Points for the guild and Guild Tokens for participants. When all trials finish, every eligible member receives bonus Guild Tokens based on the guild's overall trial progress.
  2. Guild Buildings: 23 buildings upgradable with Guild Points, up to level 20 each.
    1. Guild Hall increases max members. Builder's Hall, Treasury, and Archives boost Guild Points, Guild Token rewards, and Guild Experience.
    2. Skilling and Combat Encampments add trial sign-up slots.
    3. 17 trial buildings boost members' skills and combat attributes during your guild's trials.
  3. Shrines and Shrine Buffs:
    1. 5 shrines (Force, Tempo, Spirit, Rarity, Scholar), each unlocking one skilling and one combat buff. Each shrine level raises the max level of its buffs.
    2. Shrine buffs are permanent personal buffs purchased in the Guild Shop with Guild Tokens and Guild Credits. Refunding a level returns 80% of its cost.
    3. Buff levels stay with your character, but are capped by your current guild's shrine levels and inactive without a guild.
  4. Guild Credits: exchange items for 8 colors of Guild Credits in the Guild Shop, each color converted from a different item category (skilling resources, combat materials, consumables, essences, equipment, rare items, and more).
  5. Guild Icons: exchanged items also unlock guild icons. An item's icon unlocks at 1,000 exchanged and gains glow colors at 10K, 100K, and beyond. The leader can set the guild's icon for 100 Guild Points or rename the guild for 5,000 Guild Points.
  6. Guild Types: guilds are Casual by default and can recruit players of any game mode. The leader can permanently commit to the Standard or Ironcow type, restricting recruitment to matching characters and increasing the Guild Hall bonus from 2 to 5 member slots per level.
  7. Guild Applications: guilds now have public profiles with a recruitment message, minimum Total Level requirement, and optional auto-accept. Players can apply directly from a guild's profile.
  8. Members tab: new Contributions view showing each member's guild XP, weekly XP, trial sign-ups, and trial tiers cleared.
  9. Guilds leaderboard: new Buildings, Shrines, and Guild Points categories.
  10. Leadership: if the guild leader has been inactive for 30+ days, the next member in line can claim leadership.
  11. View the Guild tab in the Game Guide for more details.
Cowbell Store:
  1. New cosmetic types: Avatar Backgrounds and Avatar Borders, displayed behind and around your avatar. 7 of each are available in the Cowbell Store, purchasable with Cowbells and Supporter Points.
  2. Added 7 equipment-themed avatar outfits to the Cowbell Store.
  3. Added the Labyrinth chat icon to the Cowbell Store.
  4. Supporter Points can now be exchanged for Cowbells in the Cowbell Store: 10 points for a Cowbell, or 200 points for a tradable Bag Of 10 Cowbells.
Localization:
  1. The game is now available in 7 additional languages: Español, Français, Português, 繁體中文, 日本語, 한국어, and Русский. Thanks to all the players who contributed translation feedback!
Chat:
  1. Chat Translation: chat messages can now be automatically translated into your language. Set your translation language in Settings -> Chat, and mark languages you understand to leave them untranslated.
  2. Renamed General chat to Global and added an English language channel.
  3. You can now join a second language channel.
  4. Chat settings moved to their own tab in Settings.
QOL:
  1. Reorder queued actions with drag-and-drop.
  2. Reorder loadouts with drag-and-drop.
  3. Customize the order of the navigation menu in Settings -> Game -> Navigation Menu.
  4. The Loot Tracker is now the Loot & XP Log and tracks experience gained per skill alongside loot.

1777165200000.heading: Medium Patch - Labyrinth Improvements, Steam Client Update, and More!
1777165200000.content:
Labyrinth:
  1. Added 9 permanent Labyrinth Shop buff upgrades.
  2. Scroll buffs no longer function inside the Labyrinth.
  3. Scrolls can now be decomposed into 5 Labyrinth Tokens with Alchemy.
  4. Labyrinth full automation upgrade limit increased from 12 to 15.
  5. Labyrinth now requires a total level of 1000+ to enter. Existing characters who have entered labyrinth previously are unaffected.
  6. Travel time between rooms reduced from 3s to 1s.
  7. Rush to exit now takes a more optimal route that also tries to maximize the number of visible rooms revealed.
  8. Enhancing rooms now have a fixed target of +5, making difficulty consistent with other skilling rooms.
  9. Labyrinth item and XP gains are now included in offline progress tracking.
  10. Loot tracker for Labyrinth condensed to 1 row per full run.
  11. "Dungeon Refiner" achievement renamed to "Refiner" and now includes labyrinth items. (Anyone who qualifies will be granted the achievement.)
  12. Mimic minimum attack interval lowered from 1s to 0.5s, allowing Bulwark to be more effective against it.
Steam Client:
  1. Redesigned the built-in extension manager for installing and updating userscripts. It should support all browser extensions directly now.
    1. Note: You have to reinstall existing extensions due to format changes. Your previous scripts can still be found under %APPDATA%\milky-way-idle\scripts\ if you need to reference them.
  2. Steam client remembers window position and size across restarts.
  3. Fixed a rare issue where some players could see a white screen due to an anti-virus or computer security policy false positive.
Rebalance:
  1. Sinister Cape: Defensive Damage 6% added.
  2. Anchorbound Plate Body: Defensive Damage 6% added.
  3. Anchorbound Plate Legs: Defensive Damage 5% added.
  4. Fighter Necklace: Defensive Damage 4% added.
  5. Philosopher's Necklace: Defensive Damage 4% added.
  6. Sundering Crossbow: Ranged Accuracy increased from 145% to 160%, Ranged Damage increased from 118% to 120%.
  7. Soul Hunter Crossbow: Ranged Accuracy increased from 132% to 140%.
QOL:
  1. The game can be added to the home screen and launched like an app.
  2. Allow queuing Party Ready multiple times for certain rare use cases.
  3. Zero-cooldown abilities are now automatically placed in the last ability slot in battle so they don't block other abilities if positioned incorrectly.
UI:
  1. Added a global Help chat channel.
  2. Misclick Warnings: warns if the protection item used is priced much higher than the cheapest valid option, and warns if coinifying an expensive item at a loss.
  3. Queueing actions will no longer open the full action list (which could get in the way).
Other:
  1. Added Rule 2.4 forbidding coordinated market manipulation (such as pump and dump schemes).
  2. Expanded FAQ in Game Guide with a Support section and additional questions.
Bug Fix:
  1. Defensive damage from Defense Coffee now updates on the first battle instead of the second.
  2. Correctly validate whether the character has enough items to initiate enhancing when the protection item and the cost item are the same.
  3. Header success rate display now correctly reads 100% when using Philosopher's Mirror.
  4. Personal buff expiration no longer briefly displays 0s for a few extra minutes.
  5. Can now add labyrinth to the action queue when only party combat is running.

1773043200000.heading: Minor Patch - QOL Improvements and Bug Fixes
1773043200000.content:
QOL:
  1. Added option to rush for exit up to floor X in automation tab.
  2. Added labyrinth setting to allow manual pathing to ignore skip thresholds.
  3. Increased labyrinth max entries from 3 to 5.
  4. Increased torch upgrade cap to level 15 (400 max).
  5. Increased full automation cap to level 12.
UI:
  1. Marketplace can now open in a popup when accessed from other pages.
  2. Seals are renamed to Scrolls.
  3. Moved scrolls to separate category in inventory with more informative tooltips.
  4. Adjusted escape button layout and added extra confirmation to reduce misclicks. Confirmation will appear if additional progress is still possible (have torches, clearable rooms, etc).
  5. Added labyrinth timer to top progress bar.
  6. Added buffed combat level to labyrinth info.
Rebalance:
  1. Gourmet Scrolls bonus changed from 16% to 10%. They now also grant double progress to cooking/brewing in labyrinth.
Cosmetics:
  1. Added chat icons for labyrinth monsters.
  2. Added siren avatar and outfit.
Bug Fix:
  1. Correctly grant labyrinth entry immediately when cooldown upgrade is purchased with less than 4 hours until next entry.
  2. Skill level ups inside labyrinth now correctly show chat level up message.
  3. Fixed mobile item tooltips appearing unnecessarily when tapping to open the item menu.
  4. Labyrinth skilling room timer will correctly sync with server time when displayed.
  5. Fixed uncommon issue where using shroud may cause regular combat battle to be stopped.
  6. Personal buff expiration now updates in real time instead of sometimes having a short delay.
  7. Pathfinder boots tailoring XP now matches other boots.
  8. Fixed translation inconsistencies related to labyrinth content.

1771822800000.heading: Hotfix
1771822800000.content:
Bug Fix:
  1. Allow queuing party ready to correctly include loadouts.
  2. Correctly remove personal buffs when expired in combat.
  3. Crate costs and XP rates were miscalculated and significantly differed from other Labyrinth supplies. Food Crates have increased by 6x in cost and Tea and Coffee Crates by 3.3x. Existing items will be reduced proportionally and rounded up to maintain the same total value as before. XP and action times have also been adjusted to match the XP rates of other labyrinth supplies. Marketplace sell listings for crates are immediately set to expired since they are no longer priced correctly, and unclaimed items are also adjusted by the same proportion.
  4. Newly purchased Moo Pass should correctly grant +3 labyrinth path length.
  5. Correctly calculate effective combat level in labyrinth. Previously it was using an incorrect formula that is slightly different from actual combat level.
Rebalance:
  1. Lowered Mimic HP by 8% and Smash evasion by 15%.
UI:
  1. Added labyrinth monster weakness to their tooltips.
  2. Added confirmation dialog when descending to the next labyrinth floor to avoid misclicks.
  3. Minor improvements to labyrinth instruction and game guide.

1771718400000.heading: Major Patch - Labyrinth and QOL Improvements
1771718400000.content:
Labyrinth
  1. The Labyrinth consists of floors of skilling and combat challenges with increasing difficulty. Navigate through a grid of rooms to progress, collect loot, and advance to deeper floors.
    1. Each floor contains skilling challenges, combat encounters, treasure rooms, and a floor exit.
    2. Bring supplies into each run: Torches, Shrouds, Beacons, and Crates. Each comes in three tiers.
    3. Earn Labyrinth Tokens, Purdora's Boxes, and Labyrinth Refinement Chests. Rewards increase with floor depth. Spend tokens in the Labyrinth Shop on upgrades and unique items.
    4. Item rewards include: 4 Skilling Capes, 3 Combat Boots, and Scrolls which is a new type of item that grant timed personal buffs.
    5. Automation system with per-room loadouts, skip thresholds, and full auto-pathing.
    6. 4 new achievements related to the Labyrinth. Achievement tier buffs will require the completion of newly added achievements, although they should not be too difficult for those who have the rest of the tier completed.
    7. New collection items and bestiary entries for labyrinth monsters and items.
    8. Labyrinth points and labyrinth depth leaderboard.
    9. See the Game Guide for more details.
Seasonal:
  1. New Spring Festival seasonal cosmetics: chat icon, avatar, and avatar outfit.
QOL:
  1. Browser notifications for various events for Steam and browser players (not available on mobile). Configurable in Settings.
  2. Allow "Start Now" to optionally insert actions to the front of the action queue instead of replacing it. Enable this in Settings -> Game.
  3. Queue Party Ready can now be queued after solo actions, so you can automatically return to party combat without being present.
  4. Unrefine alchemy action: converts refined equipment back to its base version, preserving enhancement level and returning half of the refinement shards.
  5. Party combat experience debuff now requires a minimum of 10 level gap before it can take effect. This prevents low level players partying together from being debuffed.
  6. Increased the number of buyable loadout slots by 10.
UI:
  1. Special chat icons now display in most places with character names, not only in chat.
  2. Level up messages in general chat now only show for level 150+ or TL2000+, as lower level messages were too spammy.
Other:
  1. Creator code program: revenue share for partnered content creators. If you are a content creator and are interested in promoting the game, email contact@milkywayidle.com or contact us on Discord.
  2. Hourly marketplace.json API data now includes volume and average price.

1765252800000.heading: Bug Fixes
1765252800000.content:
1. Correctly calculate bestiary points at breakpoints.
  2. Correctly grant achievement buffs when completing achievements while offline.
  3. Add T90 pouch to collection for ironcows who have it as it was missed in the ironcow backfill previously.
  4. Fix house build button position.
  5. Translate achievement tier labels in profile.
  6. Fix some other minor bugs.

1764979200000.heading: Major Patch - Achievements, Collection, Bestiary, And More
1764979200000.content:
Features and Content:
  1. Achievements:
    1. 73 Achievements spanning 6 tiers of difficulty.
    2. Completing each tier grants permanent buffs.
    3. Achievements are synced with Steam achievements when playing from Steam client.
    4. View other players' achievements on their profile.
    5. Most achievements are not retroactive and must be earned again.
    6. Ironcow-only: Elite and Champion tier item-related achievements will be retroactive if you have earned the item previously.
  2. Collection:
    1. Track all items you have collected throughout your adventure.
    2. Collecting 1, 10, 100, 1K, 10K, etc of each item grants increasing collect points.
    3. Earn milestone rewards based on collection points.
    4. Collection progress is displayed on leaderboards.
    5. Share collected items in chat by linking them.
    6. Marketplace and General Shop purchased items do not count toward collection.
    7. Task badges and back slot items owned will be retroactively added to your collection.
    8. Ironcow-only: a number of rare items owned will be retroactively added to your collection.
  3. Bestiary:
    1. Track all monsters you have defeated.
    2. Defeating 1, 10, 100, 1K, 10K, etc of each monster grants increasing bestiary points.
    3. Earn milestone rewards based on bestiary points.
    4. Bestiary progress is displayed on leaderboards.
    5. Higher tier monsters count as 1 extra defeat per tier.
    6. When in a party, bestiary credit is divided among party members.
  4. Philosopher's Mirror: Allows merging equipment to enhance with 100% success. (ex: +13 item, +14 item, and Philosopher's Mirror -> +15 item)
  5. Community buffs can now be purchased using supporter points.
QOL:
  1. Loadouts are now editable - modify saved loadouts directly without recreating them.
  2. Combat triggers can now be saved and loaded with combat loadouts.
  3. Click your avatar in the top right corner for a quick menu for faster navigation.
  4. Market listings now show all of your own listings even if they are below the top 20.
  5. Added real-time market price warnings when creating listings significantly above estimated fair price. (experimental)
  6. Added double confirmation for unreadying from party combat.
Bug Fix:
  1. Fixed loadout visual bug incorrectly displaying missing item.
  2. Fixed various localization and translation issues.
Other:
  1. Custom cosmetic requests will no longer be available after end of the year.

1760396400000.heading: Minor Patch - Halloween, QOL, and Miscellaneous Updates
1760396400000.content:
Features and Content:
  1. Added Halloween cosmetics to the Cowbell Store. Seasonal cosmetics now display remaining duration in the store.
  2. Added new chat icons to the Cowbell Store: Jackalope, Magician, Enchanted Queen, and Anchor Shark.
  3. Increased the number of buyable loadout slots with additional lower-priced options.
UI:
  1. MooPass icon now displays the number of days remaining until expiration. A red border will appear when less than 1 day remains.
  2. Added a trade rules acknowledgment popup when first visiting the marketplace.
  3. Small adjustments to UI including tab design and layout.
  4. Added captcha verification that may occasionally appear when connecting to the game as an anti-goldfarm measure.
QOL:
  1. Loadouts can now automatically equip the highest enhanced item of the same type. This option is enabled by default.
  2. Trainee Charm now has a sell value of 250,000 coins but can no longer be coinified. This allows players to sell their excess charms after enhancing.
Bug Fix:
  1. Fixed an issue where the whisper command would be unexpectedly cleared.
  2. Fixed various other minor visual and localization bugs.
Other:
  1. Removed Guardian Aura to Mystic Aura exchange from the Cowbell Store.
  2. Backend preparations for upcoming Achievement and Labyrinth features.
  3. Reduced the size of locally saved browser data and added detection for when localStorage is full (often caused by browser extensions). A warning will display at 75% capacity, and the game will automatically delete large fields (over 1MB) at 95% capacity to maintain functionality.
  4. Combat consumables and combat trigger data are now included in shareable character profiles for exporting to combat simulation tools. This data is not displayed in the profile UI.
  5. Optimized server restart speed.

1755644400000.heading: Hotfix
1755644400000.content:
Bug Fix:
  1. Fixed combat level calculated incorrectly in battle under certain conditions, occasionally resulting in incorrect debuffs for party members.
  2. Magician's Hat ★ now has the correct level requirement of 110.
Balancing:
  1. Enchanted Bishop's defensive damage reduced as the retaliation damage was still too high making it more difficult than it should be.
  2. Refined equipment bonus increased from 5% to 8% (16% for back slot equipment) to allow it to be cost effective for more players.

1755558000000.heading: Major Patch - Combat Rework, Charms, and Refined Equipment
1755558000000.content:
Combat Rework
  1. All combat styles now require the Attack skill for accuracy. Players will now receive Attack experience equal to 15% of their existing Ranged experience and 12% of their Magic experience. This adjustment is based on leaderboard data to ensure comparable total experience across the three combat styles.
  2. Bonus magic evasion from the Ranged skill and bonus resistances from the Magic skill have been moved to the Defense skill.
  3. The Power skill has been renamed to Melee.
  4. The Regal Sword can now parry damage for party members.
  5. Pestilent Shot has been reworked to debuff Armor/Resistances instead of HP/MP regeneration.
  6. Critical rate and other buffs have been added to Ranged equipment to compensate for the loss of accuracy.
  7. Magic damaging abilities now scale by 0.5% per ability level instead of 1%.
  8. Rippling Trident now restores 10 MP when Ripple activates.
  9. Ability damage and other buffs have been added to Magic equipment to compensate for the loss of accuracy and reduced ability scaling.
  10. Bulwark:
    1. Bulwark has been reworked to include a new stat, Defensive Damage, significantly increasing its damage output.
    2. Spiked Shell has been reworked to include both physical and elemental thorns, scaling based on both Defensive Damage and Armor/Resistances.
    3. Arcane Reflection has been converted to Retribution, granting a Retaliation buff that reflects a percentage of incoming damage as a Smash attack.
    4. Shield Bash's Armor scaling has been increased from 60% to 70%.
  11. Auras:
    1. Aqua and Flame Auras have been converted into Mystic Aura, which grants an amplify bonus to all elements.
    2. Sylvan Aura has been converted into Guardian Aura, which grants healing amplify, evasion, Armor, and Resistances. Guardian Aura can temporarily be traded into Mystic Aura in the Shop.
    3. Base Aura stats have been adjusted.
    4. Each Aura now additionally scales with the caster's corresponding skill: Speed Aura → Attack, Guardian Aura → Defense, Fierce Aura → Melee, Critical Aura → Ranged, Mystic Aura → Magic.
    5. All blue ability experience will be reverted into books at a rate of 1 book per 500 experience. Any remaining experience below 500 will stay on the ability. This is a one-time conversion.
  12. A fixed amount of combat experience is now granted based on the monster defeated, with an increasing Survival XP bonus the longer the fight lasts. 30% of the experience is granted to the primary training skill determined by your weapon. The distribution of the remaining 70% can be chosen using the new Charm equipment. This system allows players to optimize their experience distribution across preferred skills. Experience rates are balanced so that players can gain more in their primary skill, but potentially less total experience overall.
  13. Monsters no longer have base regeneration.
  14. Monsters will now enrage every 3 minutes, increasing their accuracy and damage by 10% (stacking up to 10 times). Bosses use a 10-minute timer instead of 3 minutes.
  15. Some monster stats have been adjusted to balance around these changes.
  16. Combat level formula has been updated.
  17. Party combat experience is now divided equally among all party members, regardless of damage contribution. Party members who are both at least 20% below and at least 10 levels below the highest combat level will receive an experience and drop debuff, increasing by 3% for each 1% level difference beyond the threshold.
  18. All combat styles have been balanced to deal comparable damage and achieve similar experience rates.
Features and Content:
  1. Combat Zones now have selectable difficulty tiers ranging from T0 to T5. T0 corresponds to the previous normal zones, while T2 is slightly above the previous Elite zones. Higher difficulty grants additional experience, increased drops, and some unique drops.
  2. Charms:
    1. Charms are new equipment that can be equipped in the Charm slot to grant bonus experience to a specific skill. They can also be used to focus experience toward that skill in combat.
    2. Trainee Charms can be purchased from the Shop for 250K.
    3. Higher-tier non-combat Charms can be crafted using Skilling Essences.
    4. Higher-tier combat Charms can be obtained as drops from higher-tier combat zones.
    5. Charms can be combined into higher tiers using the Crafting skill.
    6. Charms can be enhanced and grant 5x the normal enhancement bonus, like other jewelry equipment.
  3. Dungeons now have higher difficulty tiers, up to T2.
  4. Refinement:
    1. T1 and T2 dungeons drop Refinement Chests, which are opened using the same Chest Keys to obtain Refinement Shards.
    2. Refinement Shards can be used to upgrade Lv.95 equipment and back-slot items from their respective dungeons.
    3. Refined equipment grants +5% extra stats (+10% for back-slot items) and increases weapon special effects.
    4. Refining an existing item will transfer 100% of enhancement levels.
  5. Enhancement scaling has been moderately increased for +11 and higher.
  6. Supporter chat icons (different tiers of Cowbells) have been added to the Cowbell Store. They can be purchased with supporter points and will display as a special second icon.
  7. Default loadout slot count increased from 2 to 3.
Other:
  1. Official market data now updates every 1 hour instead of every 6 hours.

1749250800000.heading: Medium Patch - Chat Report System and QOL Improvements
1749250800000.content:
Features and Content:
  1. Chat Report System: Players can now report rule-breaking messages in the chat.
    1. Reports are sent to moderators for review and potential action.
    2. Automod may be applied to messages that receive enough reports. Penalty can still be adjusted after manual review.
    3. Sending correct reports will increase the weight of your future reports and incorrect reports will significantly reduce it.
  2. You can now delete character in Settings.
  3. Added special icons (Contributor, Bug Finder, etc) that can be granted to players for significant contributions. They will display as a second icon.
QOL:
  1. You can now reorder action queues.
  2. You can now link market listings in chat. This should improve trade chat experience with different languages.
Other:
  1. Added official market data that can be accessed at https://www.milkywayidle.com/game_data/marketplace.json. This is updated every 6 hours (with some randomness and delay to prevent manipulation) and contains a snapshot of the top ask and bid prices for each item.
  2. Removed @mod command from the chat as it is no longer needed.
  3. Server optimizations to improve backend performance and support additional players.

1747609200000.heading: Medium Patch - MooPass and Other Improvements
1747609200000.content:
Features and Content:
  1. MooPass: an optional membership that grants a number of helpful but non-essential perks.
    1. Available for purchase in the Cowbell Store with either Cowbells or real money.
    2. All characters can claim 14 days of MooPass for free. Additional 14 days granted to all existing characters.
    3. +5% XP buff. This is limited to Standard characters only to allow Ironcow characters to not be significantly affected by paid content.
    4. +10 Hour Offline Progress Limit
    5. +6 Market Listing Limit
    6. +1 Action Queue Limit
    7. +8 Task Slot Limit
    8. +1 Free Task Reroll (per task)
    9. Loot Tracker of last 20 activities. This is found below the Cowbell Store in the menu.
    10. Golden Avatar Border
Steam:
  1. Updated Steam client and moved local save data to a more stable file location. For the first time after patching, wou may need to re-login and reconfigure extensions.
QOL:
  1. Market listing max price increased from 10B to 100B.
  2. Market listing prices that are in shortened format will now be color coded based on their primary unit (K, M, B, T) to reduce chance of misreading prices.
  3. Added /r chat command to reply to the last whisper.
  4. Added stricter spam protection for excessive duplicate chat messages from 1 or more players.
Bug Fixes:
  1. Fixed issue where enhancing essence drop rate was 33% lower than displayed. (I buffed it at some point but only updated the client and forgot the backend)
  2. Fixed issue with unread whispers count not showing up when reentering the game.
  3. Cheese type Plate Body Auto Attack Damage now correctly match Plate Legs bonuses. Special equipment are unaffected.
  4. Correctly translate party chat links for Chinese language.
  5. Allow leaderboard tabs on mobile to wrap to second line so the Steam categories do not cause display overflow.
Other:
  1. Removed seasonal Anniversary Purple and OG Jerry chat icons.
  2. Removed Brown/White Key Fragment exchange from the dungeon shop as it was meant to be temporary.
  3. Market taxes now round up instead of round down to reduce monopolization of low-value item trading.

1744930800000.heading: Major Patch - Pirate Cove Dungeon and More
1744930800000.content:
Features and Content:
  1. Pirate Cove Dungeon:
    1. New T95 Magic Weapons: Rippling Trident, Blooming Trident, Blazing Trident.
    2. New T95 Armors: Anchorbound Plate Body/Legs, Maelstrom Plate Body/Legs, Kraken Tunic/Chaps, Corsair Helmet, Marksman Bracers.
    3. New Abilities: Shield Bash (also added to Chimerical Den), Fracturing Impact, Life Drain.
    4. Alchemy recipes added for the new items. All dungeon ability book transmutes have been normalized to 50% success.
  2. Furious Spear: New T95 spear added to Enchanted Fortress.
  3. Upgrading equipment with production skills will now save 70% of the enhancement levels. Some T95 equipment recipes are adjusted to be upgraded from lower tier variants.
  4. Added 4th character slot. You can only have up to 3 Ironcows.
  5. Added Steam leaderboard for characters created after the Steam release and is also linked to Steam. This is only visible on qualifying characters.
  6. Increased guild member slots from 25 + level/4 to 30 + level/3.
Cosmetics:
  1. Seasonal(2 year anniversary): Anniversary Purple, OG Jerry chat icons.
  2. Added new chat icons based on a small selection of dungeon bosses.
  3. Added 2 new Pirate Cove themed avatars and outfits.
  4. Custom cosmetics are changed to require spending supporter points and cowbells. We are simply overwhelmed by requests due to the huge increase in player count and unfortunately cannot continue supporting them as gifts. The new pricing will take effect on May 1st. Any requests initiated before end of April will be granted based on the previous supporter point requirements.
Rebalancing:
  1. Adjusted recipe of Chimerical Chest Key and Enchanted Chest Key so that their Brown and White Key Fragments are swapped. This prevents Enchanted Fortress and Pirate Cove from sharing 3/4 key fragments. You can temporarily freely swap between Brown and White Key Fragments in the dungeon shop until the next patch.
  2. Added auto attack damage to all melee armor to bring melee damage closer to the other styles.
  3. Increased bulwark accuracy and damage
  4. Added melee accuracy to Colossus Plate Body/Legs
  5. Crippling Slash: Reworked to damage all enemies.
  6. Maim: Damaged over time duration reduced from 15s to 9s. Damage taken debuff increased from 0% to 8%.
  7. Frost Surge: Evasion debuff lowered from 15% to 10%.
  8. Firestorm: Damage over time duration reduced from 10s to 6s.
  9. Smoke Burst: Accuracy debuff lowered from 20% to 15%. Evasion debuff increased from 0% to 15%.
  10. Golem Cave: Monster defense level reduced by 10-20.
  11. Twilight Zone: Monster fire resistance reduced by 20.
UI:
  1. Added a new setting to hide General chat system messages.
  2. Disabled party linking in General, Trade, and Beginner chat.
  3. Added double confirmation to some buttons that are troublesome when misclicked.
  4. Added inactive days count to guild and friend list.
  5. Changed "Start" button text on alchemy and enhancing to be more obvious about what action it is.
Bug Fixes:
  1. Do not prevent making an instant market order when you have 3 open listings on the item.
  2. Fixed issue that may cause small number of player's actions to stop after a server restart.
  3. Fixed a rare bug that may cause server crash.
Other:
  1. Blocking characters will prevent party join (based on leader) and guild invite.
  2. Server and client optimizations to speed up server restart, task generation, scroll performance, and more.
  3. Added a feature to allow admin to post an announcement bar for important messages.
  4. Localization improvements.

1741906800000.heading: Minor Patch - Anti-Fraud Measures and Bug Fixes
1741906800000.content:
Anti-Fraud:
  1. Due to a few instances of recent fraudulent purchases, added anti-fraud measure where player's first time Cowbell purchase may trigger a 72-hour restriction on selling Bags of Cowbells on the market. You will be notified prior to purchasing.
Bug Fix:
  1. Fixed an issue where under extremely rare conditions a player may get stuck in a nonexistent party combat action.
  2. Fixed additional translation inconsistencies with some item names and their corresponding actions.
Other:
  1. Backend changes to improve database connection stability.

1740913200000.heading: Major Patch - Chinese Localization and Final Preparations for Steam Early Access Release
1740913200000.content:
UI:
  1. Added Chinese localization.
    1. The language will be automatically selected based on your browser language.
    2. Players can also manually change the display language in [Settings] -> [Game] or from the home page.
    3. A few items have not yet been translated, including most of news/patch notes, terms of use, and privacy policy.
    4. If you see any translation issues or bugged display text, please report them in the #bug-reports channel on Discord.
  2. Updated popup modals to all be centered on the entire game screen.
Other:
  1. Removed seasonal Spring Festival cosmetics from the Cowbell Store.
  2. Additional adjustments to Steam integration.
  3. Privacy policy have been updated to include language for use of analytical and marketing cookies. This allows us to optimize marketing campaigns when attempting to run ads for Milky Way Idle on other platforms.

1737885600000.heading: Minor Patch
1737885600000.content:
Spring Festival:
  1. Spring festival chat icon, avatar, and avatar outfits available in Cowbell Store until the update after at least 3 weeks. You can continue to use them after the event ends.
UI:
  1. Send a system message every 25 skill levels at or above level 100 instead of only for levels 100 and 125.
Bug Fix:
  1. Leaderboard guild names should not be clickable.
  2. Improved chat message URL link parsing to be more accurate.
  3. Fixed an issue where players get logged out if the database goes offline during maintenance.
  4. Fixed an issue where some uncommon conditions cause player combat to not correctly stop after running out of offline progress.
Other:
  1. Added @mod and @mods chat commands to notify moderators for emergencies.
  2. Implemented authentication and payment changes in the background to support upcoming Steam release.

gameGuideContent.faqContent:
General Q: How does offline progress work? A: Your character continues to make progress even when you're offline. A new player gets up to 10 hours of offline progress anytime you close the browser or go offline. You can extend this time with convenience upgrades available in the Cowbell Store or through MooPass. Q: Can I log in from another device? A: If you have registered an account, you can log in from any device using your email and password. If you are playing as a guest, you can find your guest password in Settings and use it to log in with your username. Q: Can I play the game without an internet connection or as a single player? A: No, you must be connected to the internet to play the game. However, you do continue to make progress while offline for up to 10 hours by default. If you prefer to not interact with other players, you can collapse the chat and choose not to use the marketplace. Q: Can I change my username? A: Yes, you can change your username by going to the Cowbell Store and clicking on the "Name Change" tab. It costs 500 Cowbells to change your username. Your immediate previous name will be reserved to your character. Q: How can I get a chat icon or different name color? A: You can purchase chat icons and name colors from the Cowbell Store using Cowbells. You can change your displayed icon and color in Settings. Q: How do I send a private message to another player? A: To send a private message to another player, click on their name next to their chat message and click "Whisper". You can also use the chat command "/w playerName chatMessage". Q: How do I block another player? A: To block a player and stop seeing their chat messages, click on their name in the chat and click "Block". You can also use the chat command "/block playerName". You can find your block list in the Settings menu and unblock players from the list. Q: My guild leader is no longer active, can we transfer ownership? A: Yes! If your guild leader has been inactive for at least 30 days, the most senior active member can claim leadership directly from the guild's Members tab. Each additional 2 days of inactivity extends eligibility to the next member in line. Q: How can I delete my account and its associated data? A: Account and data deletion can be requested by emailing contact@milkywayidle.com from the email address associated with the account. Gameplay Q: What is the action queue? A: The action queue is a feature that allows you to set up a sequence of actions for your character to perform. To use it, click the "Add Queue" button instead of "Start". You can reorder your queue in the menu next to your action bar at the top. Additional queue slots can be unlocked or upgraded from the Cowbell Store. Q: What are Cowbells and how can I get more? A: Cowbells are the premium currency in the game. They allow players to purchase convenience upgrades, cosmetics, community buffs that benefit the whole server, and name changes. There are four ways to get Cowbells:
  • Finish the tutorial: You will receive 80 Cowbells as a reward.
  • Rare drops: You have a chance to get Cowbells from rare loot boxes found while skilling or defeating enemies in combat.
  • Purchase from Cowbell Store: You can purchase Cowbells with real money from the Cowbell Store to help support the game. They will appear as "Bag of 10 Cowbells" in your inventory.
  • Buy from the marketplace: You can buy tradable "Bag of 10 Cowbells" with coins from other players in the Marketplace.
 Q: What are rare drops? A: Rare drops are loot boxes that can be obtained while engaging in different activities in the game:
  • Gathering skills: You get Meteorite Caches which contain star fragments.
  • Production skills, alchemy, and enhancing: You get Artisan's Crates which contain shards of protection and gems.
  • Combat: You get Treasure Chests which contain gems.
All boxes also contain coins and occasionally Cowbells. You get larger loot boxes when doing higher-level skills or from higher-level enemies. The Rare Find stat increases the drop rate of loot boxes. Q: What are gems used for? A: Gems can be used to craft different jewelry that grants small bonuses. Additionally, you can crush gems into smaller pieces with the Crafting skill and use them to brew stronger versions of coffee and tea. Gems can be obtained from Treasure Chests in combat. Q: Where do I get tea leaves? A: You can get tea leaves from defeating enemies in combat. When viewing combat zones, you can hover over an enemy (long press on mobile) to see what items it drops. Tea leaves are an essential ingredient for brewing tea, which can buff non-combat skills. Q: What are essences? A: Essences are used to enhance special equipment with the Enhancing skill. Enemies from each combat zone drop a different type of essence. Q: Where do I get a cape? A: You can find gear for the Back slot by completing the Labyrinth (skilling capes) or Dungeons (combat capes). All capes are untradeable. Q: What does it mean when a recipe requires a decimal amount of an item? A: You need the amount rounded up to start the action, but have a chance (equal to 1 minus the decimal portion) to use one less. Example: if a craft requires 11.3 cheese, you need 12, but have a 70% chance of only using 11. Q: What should I do? What's the best way to get started? A: If you aren't sure where to start, working through the achievements is a great approach — they give you a well-rounded set of goals across every skill in the game. Support Q: How do I contact a moderator or admin? A: You can contact us by opening a ticket in the #new-ticket channel on our Discord server. Alternatively, you can also email contact@milkywayidle.com from the email address associated with your account. Please include your character name and a clear description of the issue. Q: How do I report a bug or suggest a feature? A: Bug reports are best posted in the #bug-reports channel on our Discord. Please follow the bug report template in the channel. Screenshots or short clips are very helpful. You can also email us at contact@milkywayidle.com. Q: I lost items or coins because of a mistake. Can I get them back? A: As a general rule, we do not reverse losses caused by player mistakes. That said, there are some exceptions where it's worth reporting the incident to us:
  • Accidental trades involving excessive wealth transfer: Please report these to us. Reporting helps us confirm the trade was not an intentional attempt to break the rules (such as real-money trading or account boosting), which helps avoid penalties on the accounts involved. For very large losses, we may also assist with contacting the other party in the trade to try to reverse it, though there are no guarantees.
  • Exceptionally large non-market losses (usually in the billions): You may request a review of the events. If we confirm it was a genuine mistake, we can reverse the loss for a fee of 500M coins. The fee is there to discourage requests for minor losses and keep volume manageable. For losses that involve randomness, the eligible amount is discounted based on how suboptimal the action was. For example, a regular enhancing failure while using the optimal enhancing method will never qualify, since that outcome is a normal part of the system.
gameGuideContent.gatheringContent:
Milking Milking magical cows yields different types of milk, which can be used in a various ways:
  • Cheesesmithing: Milk can be turned into cheese, which can then be used to craft melee equipment or skilling tools.
  • Cooking: Milk is an essential ingredient for many recipes.
  • Brewing: Milk is used in a small number of coffee and tea recipes.
 You can help magical cows produce milk faster by equipping a brush.
Foraging Foraging allows you to gather different resources from various areas. You can forage for a specific item in an area or forage the entire area to get a bit of everything. Foraged resources can be used in:
  • Cooking: Eggs, wheat, sugar, berries, and fruits are essential ingredients for many recipes.
  • Brewing: Berries, fruits, and coffee beans are used to brew coffee and tea.
  • Tailoring: Flax, bamboo branches, cocoons, and other materials can be processed into fabric to make magical clothing.
 You can increase foraging speed by equipping shears.
Woodcutting You can chop logs from different kinds of trees. Logs can be used in:
  • Cheesesmithing: Logs are part of the recipe for making a number of melee weapons and skilling tools.
  • Crafting: Logs can be processed into lumber to craft ranged and magic weapons.
 You can increase woodcutting speed by equipping a hatchet.
Level Bonus You gain 1% efficiency bonus for each level you have above the action's level requirement.
gameGuideContent.productionContent:
Cheesesmithing Cheesesmithing is the process of creating melee equipment and skilling tools. Milk is processed into different tiers of cheese. The cheese is then used (sometimes in combination with other resources) to craft equipment. Equipment can be upgraded into higher tiers as you level up. You can increase cheesesmithing speed by equipping a hammer.
Crafting Crafting produces a variety of items, including ranged and magic weapons, jewelry, and other special resources. Logs can be processed into different tiers of lumber, which is then used to craft ranged and magic weapons. Jewelry can be crafted using star fragments and gems, which are rare drops found while gathering or from combat. You can increase crafting speed by equipping a chisel.
Tailoring Tailoring produces ranged and magic clothing as well as pouches. Raw resources from foraging, such as flax, bamboo branches, and cocoons, can be processed into fabric. Hides from combat enemies can also be processed into leather. Fabric is primarily used to make magic clothing, such as robes and hats, while leather is used to make ranged clothing, such as leather armor and boots. In addition to clothing, you can craft pouches which increase your maximum HP and MP in combat. Pouches also provide additional consumable slots for both skilling and combat. You can increase sewing speed by equipping a needle.
Cooking Cooking produces food that can be used during combat. Donuts and cakes restore HP, while gummies and yogurt restore MP. You can increase cooking speed by equipping a spatula.
Brewing Brewing produces drinks that provide buffs with limited durations. Coffee can be consumed during combat to improve combat-related stats, while tea can be consumed to improve non-combat skills. You can increase brewing speed by equipping a pot.
Level Bonus You gain 1% efficiency bonus for each level you have above the action's level requirement.
gameGuideContent.alchemyContent:
Alchemy Alchemy allows you to transform items into other items using the actions Coinify, Decompose, Transmute, or Unrefine. Each action has a different success rate, and the input item and coin cost will always be consumed regardless of success or failure.
Coinify Coinify lets you to convert items into coins. The amount of coins received is 5 times the item's sell price. The base success rate is 70%.
Decompose Decompose lets you to break down items. Equipment can be turned into their base components, and non-equipment items can be turned into skilling essences. Decomposing enhanced equipment yields bonus enhancing essences, with the quantity doubling for each enhancement level. The base success rate is 60%.
Transmute Transmute lets you to change items into other related items or rare uniques, such as the Philosopher's Stone. The base success rate varies depending on the item being transmuted.
Unrefine Unrefine lets you convert refined equipment back to its base version. The enhancement level is preserved and some of the refinement shards are returned. The base success rate is 100%.
Success Rates The base success rate depends on the alchemy action and the specific item being alchemized. If your Alchemy skill level is lower than the item's level, there will be a penalty to the success rate. The success rate can be increased by using catalysts and catalytic tea.
Catalysts Catalysts are special items that can be used to improve the success rates of Alchemy actions. One catalyst is consumed on success only. Regular catalysts can be crafted using skilling essences. Prime catalysts can be obtained by transmuting regular catalysts.
Alchemy Efficiency You gain 1% efficiency bonus for each level you have above the item's recommended level.
Instructions Here is a list of steps to follow when alchemizing items:
  1. Select the item that you want to alchemize.
  2. Select the alchemy action that you want to perform.
  3. Decide whether or not to use a catalyst. If you do, select the catalyst.
  4. Click the "Start" button and the alchemy process will begin.
gameGuideContent.enhancingContent:
Enhancing Enhancing is the process of increasing the stats of any equipment, such as armor, weapons, tools, pouches, or jewelry. When you successfully enhance an equipment, its enhancement level increases by 1. However, if the enhancement process fails, the level is reset to 0.
Success Rate The success rate of enhancing depends on several factors, including your enhancing skill level, the tier of the equipment, and the equipment's current enhancement level. Generally, the higher the tier and enhancement level of the equipment, the lower the success rate will be. Equipping an enhancer can improve your success rate.
Protection The protection mechanic is a feature that allows players to use copies of the base equipment, mirrors of protection, or crafting components (for special equipment only) to add protection to each enhancing attempt. If the enhancement fails, the equipment's level is only decreased by 1, but 1 protection item is consumed. This can be a cost-effective way to reach high enhancement levels for endgame players.
Enhancing Speed You gain 1% action speed bonus for each level you have above the item's recommended level.
Instructions Here is a list of steps to follow when enhancing equipment:
  1. Select the piece of equipment that you want to enhance.
  2. Set the target enhancement level that you would like to stop at. Be realistic about what level you can reach with your current resources.
  3. Decide whether or not to use protection. If you do then select the protection item and a minimum enhancement level where protection will be used. Generally, protection is more cost-effective when the item is at higher enhancement levels.
  4. Click the "Start" button and you will continue enhancing until you reach the target level or run out of materials.

Enhancement Bonus The bonus stats on enhanced equipment are a percentage of the base stats. The total bonus at each enhancement level is as follows:
+1: 2.0%
+2: 4.2%
+3: 6.6%
+4: 9.2%
+5: 12.0%
+6: 15.0%
+7: 18.2%
+8: 21.6%

+11: 33.4%
+12: 38.4%
+13: 44.0%
+14: 50.2%
+15: 57.0%
+16: 64.4%
+17: 72.4%
+18: 81.0%
+19: 90.2%
+20: 100% As an exception, accessories, back slot, and trinket slot enhancements receives 5x the normal bonus. For instance, a +1 enhancement on accessories is a 10% bonus.
Enhancement Base Success Rate +1: 50%
+2: 45%
+3: 45%
+4: 40%
+5: 40%
+6: 40%
+7: 35%
+8: 35%

+15: 30%

+17: 30%
+18: 30%
