gameGuideContent.combatContent:
Combat Fighting aliens can earn you coins, tea leaves, hides, essences, ability books, gems, and special items, as well as more common resources. There are enemies of varying difficulty located in different combat areas.
Equipment Wearing equipment will boost your stats in combat. You can equip items directly from the inventory or by clicking equipment slots in the equipment tab next to the inventory.
Consumables Food can be consumed to recover your HP or MP. Drinks provide limited duration buffs. Upgrading your pouch allows you to carry more food and drinks into battle.
Abilities You can learn abilities and use them in combat at the cost of MP. To unlock new abilities, you must learn them from ability books. Abilities get stronger as you level them up. You gain 0.1 XP every time it's used in combat. You can also gain a large amount of XP by consuming duplicate ability books. When multiple abilities are available for use during combat, they will be prioritized in the same order you have set them. Your Intelligence level determines how many abilities you can bring with you.
Combat Triggers Both consumables and abilities have default settings for when they will be automatically used. The settings are referred to as combat triggers, and they can be modified by clicking the gear icon below before entering the battle.
Defeat and Respawning When defeated in combat, your character will have to wait through a respawn timer before fully recovering and resuming combat automatically.
Group Combat You can create or join a party to battle in zones with multiple monsters. When all party members have pressed "Ready," the party will automatically travel to battle. Monsters will randomly attack any of the party members, and those with a higher threat stat will be targeted more frequently. Monster experience and drops will be divided with an equal chance among all players. Players who are both more than 20% lower and at least 10 levels below the highest combat level player will receive less experience and drops.
Dungeons Dungeons consists of multiple waves of higher tier elite monsters and unique dungeon bosses. They can be accessed with dungeon keys, which can be crafted after finding key fragments from bosses in regular combat zones. Up to five players can be in a dungeon party. Each person must have a key, which will be consumed after beating the final boss for the dungeon reward chest. If you complete a dungeon with fewer players, you will have a chance of looting an additional chest at the cost of an extra key. If the dungeon is not completed, you will keep your dungeon key. Deaths in dungeons will not trigger a respawn timer. You can only be revived by a party member. If all party members are dead, the dungeon run is considered failed and you will restart at wave 1.
Skills You have 7 combat skills that can be leveled up:
  • Stamina: Increases max HP by 10 per level.
  • Intelligence: Increases max MP by 10 per level.
  • Attack: Increases your accuracy, attack speed, and cast speed.
  • Defense: Increases your evasion, armor, elemental resistances, and defensive damage.
  • Melee: Increases your melee damage.
  • Ranged: Increases your ranged damage.
  • Magic: Increases your magic damage.

Status Effects There are status effects that can temporarily prevent you from taking certain actions:
  • Blind: Prevents using auto attacks.
  • Silence: Prevents using abilities.
  • Stun: Prevents using auto attacks, abilities, and consumables.

Stats You also have secondary combat stats that are affected by your skill levels, equipment, and buffs:
  • Combat style: Each attack has a specific style - stab, slash, smash, ranged, or magic.
  • Damage Type: Each attack deals a specific type of damage - physical, water, nature, or fire.
  • Attack Interval: How fast you can auto-attack.
  • Ability Haste: Reduces ability cooldown.
  • Accuracy: Increases your chance to successfully attack.
  • Damage: The maximum damage if an attack is successful. Auto-attack damage is random between 1 and the maximum damage.
  • Critical Hit: Critical hits always rolls maximum damage. Ranged style has passive critical chance.
  • Amplify: Increases the damage you deal.
  • Penetration: Ignores a percentage of enemy armor or resistance when attacking.
  • Evasion: Increases your chance to dodge an attack.
  • Armor: Mitigates a percentage of physical damage.
  • Resistance: Mitigates a percentage of water, nature, or fire damage.
  • Life Steal: Heals you based on the percentage of auto-attack damage you deal.
  • Mana Leech: Leeches mana based on the percentage of auto-attack damage you deal.
  • Thorn: When attacked, deals a percentage of your defensive damage back to the attacker. Damage is increased by 1% per armor or resistance (corresponding to the attack type).
  • Retaliation: When attacked, reflects a percentage of (defensive damage + incoming damage) as a smash attack back to the attacker.
  • Tenacity: Reduces chance of being blinded, silenced, or stunned.
  • Threat: Increases chance of being targeted by monsters.
  • HP/MP Regen: Recovers a percentage of your maximum HP/MP every 10 seconds.
  • Food Haste: Reduces food cooldown.
  • Drink Concentration: Increases drink effect. Reduces duration and cooldown.
  • Combat Drop Rate: Increases the drop rate of regular items. This cannot go above 100%.
  • Combat Drop Quantity: Increases quantity of regular item drops.
  • Combat Rare Find: Increases rare item drop rate.
  • Primary Training: 30% of combat experience is distributed to the primary training skill determined by your weapon.
  • Focus Training: 70% of combat experience is distributed to the focus training skill determined by your charm.
  • Combat Level: This is only for display and represents your overall combat effectiveness based on the combination of combat skill levels.

Formulas For those who enjoy math, here are the formulas for the secondary stats:
  • Max HP = 10 * (10 + Stamina)
  • Max MP = 10 * (10 + Intelligence)
  • Attack Interval = BaseInterval / (1 + (Attack / 2000)) / (1 + AttackSpeedBonus)
  • Cast Time = BaseCastTime / (1 + (Attack / 2000) + CastSpeedBonus)
  • Ability Cooldown = BaseCooldown * 100 / (100 + AbilityHaste)
  • Accuracy = (10 + Attack) * (1 + Bonus%)
  • Damage = (10 + [Melee|Ranged|Magic|Defense]) * (1 + Bonus%)
  • Bulwark Smash Damage = SmashDamage + DefensiveDamage
  • Thorn Damage = DefensiveDamage * (1 + [Armor|Resistance] / 100) * Thorn%
  • Retaliation Damage = (DefensiveDamage + MIN(AttackerPremitigatedDamage, 5 * DefensiveDamage)) * Retaliation%
  • Hit Chance = (MyAccuracy ^ 1.4) / (MyAccuracy ^ 1.4 + EnemyEvasion ^ 1.4)
  • Ranged Bonus Critical Rate = 0.3 * Hit Chance
  • Evasion = (10 + Defense) * (1 + Bonus%)
  • Armor = 0.2 * Defense + Bonus
  • Percent Physical Damage Taken = 100 / (100 + Armor)
    If Armor is negative then it's = (100 - Armor) / 100
  • Resistance = 0.2 * Defense + Bonus
  • Percent Elemental Damage Taken = 100 / (100 + Resistance)
    If Resistance is negative then it's = (100 - Resistance) / 100
  • Blind/Silence/Stun Chance = Base Chance * 100 / (100 + Tenacity)
  • Targeted By Monster Chance = MyThreat / (TeamTotalThreat)
  • Combat Level = 0.1 * (Stamina + Intelligence + Attack + Defense + MAX(Melee, Ranged, Magic)) + 0.5 * MAX(Attack, Defense, Melee, Ranged, Magic)
gameGuideContent.labyrinthContent:
Labyrinth The Labyrinth consists of floors of skilling and combat challenges. Each run takes you through floors of increasing difficulty, each consisting of a grid of rooms. Clear rooms to progress, collect loot, and advance to deeper floors. The Labyrinth unlocks at total level 1000. It is recommended to have levels in many different skills and combat styles to attempt at least half of the room types to effectively run the Labyrinth.
Entries & Cooldown You can hold up to 5 entries at a time. Entries regenerate one at a time on a cooldown timer (base 72 hours, reducible to 48 hours via upgrades). Each run costs 1 entry.
Floors & Grid Each floor is a grid of rooms. The grid size grows with floor depth:
  • Floor 1: 4x4 grid
  • Floor 2: 5x5 grid
  • Floor 3: 6x6 grid
  • Floor 4: 7x7 grid
  • Floor 5+: 8x8 grid
You start at the top-left corner and the Floor Exit is at the bottom-right. Room levels are based on the floor: starting at 20-40 and increasing by 20 levels per floor.
Room Types
  • Start Room: Your starting position on each floor.
  • Skilling Rooms: Challenge one of 10 non-combat skills. About half of challenge rooms are skilling.
  • Combat Rooms: Solo battles against labyrinth monsters. About half of challenge rooms are combat.
  • Treasure Rooms: Grant bonus Labyrinth Tokens and loot. The number of treasure rooms equals the floor number (capped at 6).
  • Floor Exit: Grants larger rewards. Unlocks the next floor.
Supplies You bring supplies into each run. Supply caps can be upgraded from the Labyrinth Shop.
  • Torches: One torch is consumed per room entered. Higher tier torches have a chance to be preserved (Basic: 0%, Advanced: 10%, Expert: 20%). You cannot enter rooms without torches. Produced with Crafting.
  • Shrouds: Instantly clear a visible challenge room without receiving XP or rewards from it. Basic Shrouds work up to level 50, Advanced up to level 100, and Expert has no level cap. Shrouds have a 2% fail chance per level above their cap. Produced with Tailoring.
  • Beacons: Reveal hidden rooms in an area. Basic reveals up to 5 rooms, Advanced up to 9 rooms, and Expert up to 13 rooms. Produced with Cheesesmithing.

Crates One crate of each type can be consumed on entry and the buffs last the entire run:
  • Tea Crate: Buffs all skilling skills. Available in 3 tiers (Basic, Advanced, Expert). Produced with Brewing.
  • Coffee Crate: Buffs all combat skills. Available in 3 tiers. Produced with Brewing.
  • Food Crate: Adds HP and MP regen. Available in 3 tiers. Produced with Cooking.

Skilling Rooms Skilling rooms have a 2 minute time limit and 10 seconds per action (base, before speed bonuses). Enhancing rooms have an 8 second base action time. You must fill a progress bar to clear the room.
  • Success Rate: 80% base, multiplicatively adjusted by -1% per level below or +0.5% per level above the room. Skill level bonuses, tea crate buffs, and other bonuses increase your effective level.
  • Double Progress Chance: Chance to double progress on a success. Equal to crate buff + gathering buff (Milking, Foraging, Woodcutting) + upgrade buff.
  • Progress: Based on work power, which is your effective skill level x (1 + efficiency), rounded down to a whole number.
  • Enhancing rooms are special: each success adds +1 enhancement level, failures subtract -1 level. You must reach the target enhancement level to clear.

Combat Rooms Combat rooms are solo battles with a 2 minute time limit. You fight one labyrinth monster scaled to the room's level.
  • Shadow Archer: Ranged attacker dealing physical damage. Weak to stab and slash melee.
  • Pyre Hunter: Ranged attacker dealing fire damage. Weak to slash and smash melee.
  • Frost Sniper: Ranged attacker dealing water damage. Weak to smash and stab melee.
  • Siren: Magic attacker dealing water damage. Weak to ranged.
  • Salamander: Melee attacker dealing fire damage. Weak to ranged.
  • Dryad: Magic attacker dealing nature damage. Weak to ranged.
  • Giant Scorpion: Melee stab attacker. Weak to water and nature magic.
  • Giant Mantis: Melee slash attacker. Weak to nature and fire magic.
  • Cyclops: Melee smash attacker. Weak to fire and water magic.
  • Mimic: Melee stab attacker with very fast attack speed. Weak to thorn and retaliation.

Rewards Rewards increase with floor level. Spend tokens in the Labyrinth Shop on upgrades and items.
  • XP: Clearing challenge rooms grants XP in the relevant skill.
  • Labyrinth Tokens: Drop from challenge rooms, treasure rooms, and floor exit.
  • Purdora's Boxes: Drop from challenge rooms, treasure rooms, and floor exit.
  • Labyrinth Refinement Chests: Drop from floor exit (floor 6+).
  • Labyrinth Essences: From Purdora's Boxes. Used to enhance labyrinth equipment.
  • Scrolls: From Purdora's Boxes. Grant timed personal buffs. (Has no effect in the Labyrinth)
  • Lodestones: From Purdora's Boxes. Used to make level 95 boots.
  • Skilling Capes: From Purdora's Boxes.
  • Refinement Shards: From Labyrinth Refinement Chests. Used to refine labyrinth capes and boots.

Labyrinth Shop Spend Labyrinth Tokens in the shop. Available upgrades:
  • Cooldown Reduction: -4 hours per level (6 levels max, reduces 72h base cooldown to 48h).
  • Torch Capacity: +20 per level (15 levels max).
  • Shroud Capacity: +1 per level (8 levels max).
  • Beacon Capacity: +1 per level (10 levels max).
  • Full Automation: +1 floor per level (15 levels max).
  • Skilling Action Speed: +1% per level (12 levels max).
  • Skilling Efficiency: +1% per level (12 levels max).
  • Skilling Success Rate: +0.5% per level (12 levels max).
  • Skilling Double Progress: +1% per level (12 levels max).
  • Combat Damage: +1% per level (12 levels max).
  • Attack Speed: +1% per level (12 levels max).
  • Cast Speed: +1% per level (12 levels max).
  • Critical Rate: +1% per level (12 levels max).
  • Experience: +1% per level (12 levels max).
Shop items include essences, scrolls, lodestones, capes, and refinement shards.
Automation The Automation tab lets you configure per-room loadouts, skip thresholds, and full automation.
  • Per-Room Loadouts: Assign a loadout to each room type (10 skilling + 10 combat). The loadout is automatically equipped when entering that room type.
  • Skip Threshold: Set a level threshold for each room type. Rooms where (room level - your effective level) ≥ the threshold are automatically skipped.
  • Max Attempts Per Room: Set how many times the system will attempt a room before it is skipped.
  • Full Automation: Must be unlocked from the Labyrinth Shop (+1 floor per level). When enabled, rooms are automatically added to the path one at a time. Priority order: immediately reachable reward rooms first, then the floor exit, then remaining non-skipped rooms. Once all clearable rooms are completed, it advances to the next floor and repeats.
  • Shroud Auto-Use: When full automation is enabled, shrouds can be set to "When Stuck" or "Don't Use" (default). "When Stuck" will automatically use a shroud when there is no path to the floor exit without using one, this includes rooms that are auto-skipped or have failed too many times.

Labyrinth Points Each room cleared awards points equal to the current floor number. Points accumulate across all runs and are tracked on the leaderboard.
Formulas Mechanics:
  • GridSize = MIN(3 + Floor, 8)
  • WorkPower = EffectiveLevel * (1 + Efficiency)
  • SkillingSuccessRate = MAX(5%, 0.80 * (1 + LevelBonus + Buffs)), where LevelBonus is -0.01 per level below RoomLevel or +0.005 per level above
  • MonsterLevel = RoomLevel
  • MonsterArmorResistances = BaseArmorResistances * (RoomLevel / 100)
  • DoubleProgressChance = CrateBuff + GatheringBuff (Milking, Foraging, Woodcutting) + UpgradeBuff
  • Experience = RoomLevel * 50

Challenge Room Rewards:
  • Token: MIN(Floor * 5%, 50%) chance to drop 1 token.
  • Purdora's Box: MIN(Floor * 1%, 10%) chance to drop 1 box. Skilling rooms drop a Skilling box; combat rooms drop a Combat box.

Treasure Room Rewards:
  • Tokens: 100% chance. Amount = MIN(Floor, 10).
  • Purdora's Boxes: MIN(Floor * 5%, 50%) chance to drop 1 of each box type.

Floor Exit Rewards:
  • Tokens: 100% chance. Amount = 5 * Floor.
  • Purdora's Boxes: 100% chance, both types (floor 4+). Average quantity of each type = (Floor - 3) / 2.
  • Refinement Chest: 100% chance (floor 6+). Average quantity = (Floor - 4) / 2.
gameGuideContent.tasksContent:
Tasks Feature After completing the tutorial, you will unlock the tasks feature. The Task Board generates random short to medium-length tasks in different skills. By completing these tasks, you can obtain rewards for your participation.
Task Board
  • Frequency: Tasks are assigned periodically, starting at one every 8 hours. Upgrades can reduce the interval to as low as 4 hours.
  • Variety: Tasks may involve gathering/production skills or defeating monsters. The generated tasks will slightly prioritize skills in which the player has a higher level.
  • Rerolls: You can reroll tasks using coins or cowbells. The cost doubles (up to a limit) with each reroll.
  • Capacity: Tasks do not expire, but there's a limit of 8 task slots. The capacity can be increased through upgrades in the cowbell store.
Rewards
  • Completing tasks rewards you with Coins and Task Tokens. A Task Point is also granted for every Task Token rewarded from tasks.
  • Accumulating 50 Task Points allows you to claim "Purple's Gift," which can be opened to obtain Coins, Task Tokens, Task Crystals, and various lootboxes.
Task Shop Task Tokens can be spent in the Task Shop for permanent upgrades or items, including:
  • Task Cooldown: Reduces the cooldown between tasks.
  • Block Slots: Allows blocking specific skills from being assigned as tasks. Combat blocking has to be unlocked at an additional cost.
  • Task Crystals: Used for crafting or upgrading task badge with the Crafting skill. Task badges provide multiplicative action speed or damage bonuses while undertaking tasks.
  • Lootboxes: Large Meteorite Cache, Large Artisan's Crate, and Large Treasure Chest.
gameGuideContent.achievementsContent:
Achievements Achievements are goals spanning many areas of the game. They are organized into tiers: Beginner, Novice, Adept, Veteran, Elite, and Champion. Completing all achievements in a tier grants buffs for the character. Tier buffs:
  • Beginner: +2% Gathering
  • Novice: +2% Wisdom
  • Adept: +2% Efficiency
  • Veteran: +2% Rare Find
  • Elite: +2% Combat Damage
  • Champion: +0.2% Enhancing Success
 Note: if new achievements are added along with new major features, they must be completed again to maintain the buff.
Collection Collection tracks all unique items you have obtained outside of Marketplace and General Shop. It also tracks successful enhancement counts for each item.
Bestiary Bestiary tracks all monsters you have defeated. It also tracks defeat counts for different tiers of each monster (T0, T1, T2, etc). Higher tier monsters grant 1 extra credit per tier (T1 gives +1, T2 gives +2, etc). When fighting in a party, you receive fractional credit based on party size.
Points Both Collection and Bestiary earn points based on how many of each item/monster you have collected/defeated:
  • 1 → +1 point
  • 10 → +2 points
  • 100 → +3 points
  • 1,000 → +4 points
  • And so on...
 Reaching point milestones unlocks rewards.
gameGuideContent.guildContent:
Guild Guilds are formed by groups of players who enjoy playing together. Beyond being social hubs, guilds offer weekly Guild Trials, guild buildings, and personal shrine buffs.
Creating a Guild You can start your own guild by investing 5 million coins and choosing a unique guild name. As the guild's creator, you automatically assume the role of the leader, granting you the highest authority within the guild. Afterward, invite other players to join your guild.
Joining a Guild You can be invited to join existing guilds, or apply directly to any guild with open applications. To find a guild to join, you can use the Recruit chat channel, where guilds actively seek new members. You can view your invitations and applications on the Guild page.
Guild Features Guilds come with several key features:
  • Guild Chat Channel: A private, self-moderated space for guild members to connect and converse.
  • Guild Notice Board: A persistent message board editable by the leader or generals to keep everyone informed.
  • Guild Levels: As members earn experience in various skills, the guild accumulates XP at a 1:1000 ratio, increased by the Archives building, contributing to the guild's level.
  • Member Slots: Guilds begin with {{defaultGuildMemberCount}} member slots and gain 1 additional slot for every {{guildLevelsPerMaxMember}} guild levels. The Guild Hall building grants additional slots.
Member Roles Guilds have a structured hierarchy with different roles and permissions. Higher rank roles automatically possess the permission of any lower rank:
  • Leader
    • Can pass leadership to another member.
    • Can set the guild's type, icon, and name.
    • Has the authority to disband the guild entirely when the guild is empty.
  • General
    • Can promote or demote any lower-ranking member.
    • Can edit the guild notice board and manage application settings.
    • Can manage guild buildings and shrines, and set the weekly trial schedule.
  • Officer
    • Can invite new members and review applications.
    • Can kick lower-ranking members out of the guild.
  • Member
    • Can view the guild overview.
    • Can view and converse in the guild chat channel.
  • Invited
    • Has no access until they accept the guild invitation
Guild Types Guilds come in three types:
  • Casual: The default type. Characters of any game mode can join. The Guild Hall building grants 2 member slots per level.
  • Standard: Only Standard characters can join. The Guild Hall building grants 5 member slots per level.
  • Ironcow: Only Ironcows can join. The Guild Hall building grants 5 member slots per level.
The leader can permanently change a Casual guild to the Standard or Ironcow type if every current member already matches that type. This can never be reversed.
Guild Trials Guild Trials are weekly group challenges and the only source of Guild Points and Guild Tokens. The guild week resets every Friday at 00:00 UTC. Each week your guild receives 4 randomly chosen skilling trials and 2 randomly chosen combat trials.
  • Sign-up: Each member can sign up for at most 1 skilling and 1 combat trial per week.
  • Capacity: Each trial has a limited number of sign-up slots, which can be raised with the Skilling and Combat Encampment buildings.
  • Eligibility: Only members who joined before the guild week started may participate.
  • Schedule: The leader and generals set a weekly start time. Skilling trials run first, and combat trials start once the skilling trials finish.
  • Duration: Each trial can run for up to 1 hour of active time during the week.
  • Mechanism: The trials will not interrupt normal character actions.
Trials climb through tiers of increasing difficulty. The first tier starts at level 100, and each tier is 10 levels higher, up to a maximum of level 300. A trial keeps climbing until the group fails a tier, runs out of time, or clears the highest tier.
Skilling Trials In a skilling trial, every signed-up member performs actions in the trial's skill, and everyone's progress feeds one shared pool. The tier is cleared when the pool is filled. Higher tiers require more total work, and each signed-up participant increases the work required by 1%. Participants use a snapshot of their chosen loadout without consumables.
Combat Trials In a combat trial, the signed-up party battles one of five encounter types (Badger, Chameleon, Jellyfish, Hedgehog, or Swarm), scaled up to the tier level. Each signed-up participant increases monster HP by 1%. Winning the battle advances the trial to the next tier, while a defeat ends the trial for the week. Combat trials grant no XP or regular loot, and food and drinks are replaced by a flat +3% HP and MP regeneration. Each incoming attack can receive at most 5 parry attempts.
Guild Points and Guild Tokens Every tier cleared awards Guild Points to the guild and Guild Tokens to that trial's participants:
  • Skilling Base Points = 200 for the first tier + 100 for each additional tier cleared
  • Combat Base Points = 400 for the first tier + 200 for each additional tier cleared
  • Guild Points Earned = BasePoints * (1 + BuildersHallBonus)
  • Guild Tokens Earned = (200 for the first tier + 100 for each additional tier cleared) * (1 + TreasuryBonus), paid to each of that trial's participants.
  • Weekly Bonus Tokens = 0.5 * TotalBasePoints * (1 + TreasuryBonus), paid to every eligible member once all trials have finished
Guild Points belong to the guild and are spent by the leader and generals on buildings, shrines, and guild customization. Both spendable and lifetime Guild Points are tracked. Lifetime points are ranked on the leaderboard. Guild Tokens are yours personally. Spend them on shrine buffs in the Guild Shop, or exchange them for guild credits.
Buildings and Shrines Guild buildings and shrines are upgraded with Guild Points by the leader and generals, each up to a maximum of level 20.
  • Guild Hall: Increases member slots.
  • Builder's Hall: Increases Guild Points earned from trials.
  • Treasury: Increases Guild Tokens earned from trials.
  • Archives: Increases guild XP gained from member XP.
  • Skilling and Combat Encampments: Increase the number of sign-up slots for every skilling or combat trial.
  • Trial Buildings: One building for each skill, similar to house rooms. Grants buffs during your guild's trials.
The five shrines have no direct effect. Each shrine level instead raises the maximum level of that shrine's two buffs.
Guild Credits Guild credits are personal currencies that come in 8 colors. Earn them by exchanging items in the Guild Shop. Each eligible item converts at its own rate into a color themed by the item's purpose:
  • Green for skilling resources
  • Brown for combat materials and ability books
  • White for consumables
  • Blue for essences
  • Purple for skilling equipment
  • Red for combat equipment
  • Silver for rare items
  • Gold for very rare items
Credits are spent together with Guild Tokens on shrine buffs. Guild Tokens can also be exchanged into credits, and exchanged items count toward unlocking guild icons.
Shrine Buffs Shrine buffs are permanent personal buffs bought with Guild Tokens and guild credits in the Guild Shop, with rising costs per level. Each shrine unlocks one skilling and one combat buff: Force boosts damage and efficiency, Tempo boosts attack and action speed, Spirit boosts HP, MP, and essence find, Rarity boosts rare find, and Scholar boosts XP.
  • A buff is only active up to your current guild's matching shrine level. If you leave your guild, your buff levels are kept but inactive; in a guild with a lower shrine level, the buff is capped but not lost.
  • You can refund buff levels for 80% of the tokens and credits spent, starting from the most recently bought level.

Guild Icons and Customization Items exchanged for guild credits are tallied toward guild icons. An item's icon unlocks once the guild has exchanged 1,000 of it, then gains higher visual tiers at each tenfold milestone after that. The leader can set the guild's icon for 100 Guild Points or rename the guild for 5,000 Guild Points.
Leadership and Inactivity If the guild leader has been inactive for 30 days, the highest-priority member (by role, then join date) becomes eligible to claim leadership. Each additional 2 days of inactivity extends eligibility to the next member in line. When leadership is claimed, the previous leader is demoted to General.

gameRulesText.content:
The rules for Milky Way Idle are designed to ensure an enjoyable and fair experience for all players. Breaking the rules would result in appropriate penalties dependent on the type and severity of the offense. Penalties include verbal warning, mute, item removal, trading ban, or account ban.

  • Account
    1. Single Account Only: Each person can only play on 1 account. Guests are also considered accounts.
    2. No Account Sharing: Do not share account with other players.
    3. No Inappropriate Name: Names should not be offensive, sexual, impersonating others, or based on well-known real-world individuals. Inappropriate name may result in mutes and forced name change.
    4. Age 13+ Only: In compliance with COPPA(Children's Online Privacy Protection Act), you must be at least age 13+ to register and play.
  • Trading
    1. No Real World Trading / Cross-Trading: Do not trade items or services within Milky Way Idle for anything outside of the game.
    2. No Boosting: Do not funnel wealth to other players. Players may receive up to 10M coins in total gifts from others. Wealth transfers over this limit, whether intentional or not, can be treated as boosting. Unintentional transfers (e.g. randomly finding an extremely underpriced item on market) can be removed. Intentional transfers will result in additional penalties based on severity.
    3. No Scamming: Do not use deception or scamming to gain items from other players. Actions will be taken against scammers given sufficient evidence. However, items lost to scams will not be refunded.
    4. No Coordinated Market Manipulation: Players may not organize, incite, or participate in coordinated group efforts to manipulate the in-game economy. Encouraging other players to artificially pump or dump specific items is strictly prohibited.
    5. Repay Loans Within 7 Days: Loans with other players are at your own risk. Loans not repaid within 7 days can be considered boosting/scamming.
  • Chat
    1. Use Respectful Language: The #1 chat rule is to respect other players. Our goal is to create a friendly community space everyone can enjoy. Please avoid intentionally antagonizing or harassing others. While the occasional use of profanity is not against the rule, please don't do so excessively, especially when directed at other players.
    2. No Discrimination: Do not use slurs, slangs, or any offensive phrases that target any person or group of people.
    3. No Illegal or Sexual Topics: Do not link or discuss illegal activities or sexual topics.
    4. Avoid Divisive Topics or Drama: Avoid divisive topics that often lead to drama or conversations inappropriate for public chat. This includes, but is not limited to, politics, religion, international conflicts, gender discussions, sexuality, mute/ban complaints, and other topics that frequently become disruptive.
    5. No Spamming: Do not spam the chat with large number of unnecessary messages, overuse CAPSLOCK, or beg others for free items.
    6. Do Not Encourage Others to Break Rules: Do not mislead or instigate other players into breaking game rules.
    7. Do Not Disclose Personal Information: Do not disclose identifying personal information about yourself, including, but not limited to, your full name, address, phone number, and email address. Furthermore, do not disclose ANY personal information about other players that they have not made public themselves, such as their name, age, or location.
    8. All Advertisements Must be in Appropriate Channels: All buying, selling, or service requests should be in trade chat. Guild/Party recruitment or seeking to join guild/party requests should be in recruit channel. Price checks are allowed in most channels. No referral links.
    9. Listen to Moderators: Moderators have the discretion to moderate chat as they see fit in order to maintain a positive environment. Please cooperate with their requests. If anyone has disagreements or complaints regarding a moderator, please submit a ticket on Discord or email contact@milkywayidle.com.
  • Bots, Scripts, and Extensions
    1. No Botting: Do not use any automation that plays the game for you.
    2. Scripts and Extensions: Any scripts or extensions must not take any actions for the player (send any requests to server). You are allowed to use them purely for information display purposes or UI improvements. (ex: Display combat summary, track drops, move buttons to different location)
  • Bugs and Exploits
    1. No Bug Abusing: Do not abuse game bugs or exploits to your advantage. Please report them on Discord.
